key_findings: OVERDUE (§7): 6 records exceed 60-day submission limit (delays: 65-120 days). All are communication type with BX2025X prefix. Record IDs: R004227, R004228, R004229, R004230, R004231, R004232.

DUPLICATE (§10): 6 invoice groups confirmed reused: INV000002(R000002,R004201), INV000005(R000005,R004202), INV000020(R000020,R004203), INV000028(R000028,R004204), INV000037(R000037,R004205), INV000055(R000055,R004206). Each pair shares same invoice with different expense dates.

SPLIT (§11): 87 candidate groups (employee+expense_type within 7 days, >=2 claims, total>=3000). 75 groups are training_fee, 7 travel_lodging, 3 business_entertainment, 2 other. Notable: E0007-E0010 have structured travel_lodging splits with BX2025X prefix in 2-3 day intervals (¥10,200-10,500 totals). Training splits widespread across regular employees.

OVER-STANDARD (§12): 2 single-transaction records: R004221 office_supplies ¥650 (std=600), R004222 communication ¥330 (std=300). Additionally: R004225 travel_lodging per-night ¥900 vs std ¥850 (D1/A tier), R004226 local_transport per-day ¥92 vs std ¥80, R004224 business_entertainment per-person ¥350 vs std ¥300, R004223 training_fee ¥3700 vs std ¥3500.

OVER-BUDGET (§13): 6 departments exceed annual budget: D001(157.8%), D002(153.0%), D003(158.6%), D004(154.3%), D005(155.6%), D006(155.5%). 4 departments within budget. Zero special_approval records exist. Budget-crossing triggers: R001388(D001), R000415(D002), R002362(D003), R003179(D004), R000541(D005), R000217(D006).
unresolved_items: 87 split candidate groups need case-by-case review: training_fee splits may be legitimate multi-day courses vs genuine splitting. Main agent should determine which are anomalies.
BX2025X prefixed records (R004201-R004232) appear as a distinct batch - need clarification on their provenance and whether they should be treated differently.
Over-standard for non-§12 types (travel_lodging, local_transport, business_entertainment, training) identified but needs main agent to confirm if these fall under §12 scope or their own policy docs.
Split claims for E0007-E0010 with BX2025X prefix (e.g. R004207-R004208 totaling ¥10,400) appear highly unusual - may need special handling.
